observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Designated General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant:
complete the “one-time cleansing” of the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) by following the disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs under Section 7.0 and the derecognition procedures for such properties under Section 8.0 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 not later than the first semester of 2025;
follow the reconciliation procedures under Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 to establish comparable and verifiable information between the accounting records and the Report on the Physical Count of PPE, and proceed with the disposition and derecognition procedures under Sections 7.0 and 8.0 not later than the first semester of 2025; and
submit to COA the Request for Relief from Property Accountability of those burned properties, if any, and the Request for Derecognition of those non-existing/missing PPEs, for which accountability/responsibility could not be determined after investigation, not later than the first semester of 2025.
We further recommended and Management also agreed that the Designated General Services Officer, together with the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal Engineer, participate in training and seminars on Property and Supply Management and Property Appraisal and Disposal to be conducted by the Commission on Audit, Regional Office XI, within the Calendar Year 2025.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed that the:
Municipal Accountant regularly verify, analyze, and validate the existence of receivables, issue demand letters to concerned parties to enforce collection of dormant accounts, and impose appropriate sanctions on delinquent borrowers, if necessary;
Municipal Accountant provide impairment loss on the P860,811.60 doubtful Other Receivables; and
Municipal Accountant evaluate the status of outstanding receivables and initiate the necessary process for write-off or derecognition of dormant accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2023-008, except for the P310,000.00 receivable from Leo Misoles for the unauthorized drawing of Check No. 1291720.

Recommendations:
We reiterated our previous recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant submit a certified list of settlements of the disallowances, along with the corresponding supporting documents and a reconciliation thereof, no later than the first semester of 2025 for all settlements covering CY 2024 and prior years.
We also recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant regularly submit, either on a quarterly or monthly basis, copies of journal entry vouchers and certified payrolls of settlements made by the persons liable to facilitate the issuance of subsequent Notices of Settlement of Suspension, Disallowance, and Charge (NSSDCs).
We finally recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Administrator issue a demand letter to the individuals liable to enforce collection and recover the outstanding receivables.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Engineer submit the necessary Certificate of Completion and Acceptance, along with the Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA), for the completed “Construction of Drainage/Canal - Yungco Area, Sto. Niño” project, costing P246,642.53, to the Municipal Accountant to ensure the timely and accurate reclassification of the project to the appropriate asset account.
We also recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant reclassify as Road Networks the completed “Construction of Drainage/Canal - Yungco Area, Sto. Niño” project, costing P246,642.53.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
Municipal Treasurer produce the disbursement voucher and supporting documents covering the unrecorded Check No. 3147316 dated March 25, 2024; and
Municipal Accountant recognize all valid book reconciling items in the financial records based on the Bank Reconciliation Statements as of December 31, 2024.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
record as Due to BIR the withholding taxes erroneously recorded as Accounts Payable in the year-end financial statements; and
deduct and withhold the tax on goods and services as soon as the amount payable to the supplier is recognized in the books.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Administrator and the Municipal Planning and Development Office undertake the following actions no later than the first semester of 2025:
address the power source issue by proceeding with the planned upgrade of the water supply system through the installation of solar power, as provided in the CY 2025 Annual Budget.
ensure the validity of the secured Deed of Donation by ensuring that it is executed as a public document, duly notarized, and that the Municipality, as the donor, expressly manifests its acceptance either in the same document or in a separate document executed during the donor’s lifetime.
Additionally, we recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Engineer require JAI Builders & Supply to complete the water system in accordance with the approved program of works within one month after the energization of the pumphouse.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Engineer and Project Inspection and Monitoring Team investigate the root causes of the identified defects and safety hazards in the completed projects and require the contractor to undertake corrective measures for projects still within the Defects Liability Period.
We also reiterated our previous recommendation and Management agreed that the Municipal Engineer and Project Inspection and Monitoring Team strictly and diligently monitor all municipal projects, whether implemented by contract or by administration, in full compliance with the verification schedule prescribed by COA Circular No. 2013-004, ensuring that inspection reports and statements of work accomplished are signed only after thorough validation.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed that the BAC, through its Chairperson, use Shopping, in the procurement of goods, only when there is an unforeseen and urgent need requiring immediate purchase and the amount does not exceed P100,000.00 or in the procurement of ordinary or regular office supplies and equipment not available in the DBM-PS facility and the amount does not exceed P200,000.00. Otherwise, use the Small Value Procurement, if necessary, in order to avoid the incurrence of an irregular expenditure.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed that the:
Municipal Budget Officer start allocating funds necessary to cover the costs associated with the transfer of land titles for real properties acquired by the Municipality in CY 2022 and prior years costing P10,902,998.41;
Municipal Assessor facilitate the transfer of titles for real properties acquired by the Municipality in CY 2022 and prior years costing P10,902,998.41; and
Municipal Assessor designate specific personnel to focus solely on processing the titling of land to ensure timely and proper completion of the required procedures.

Submit to the Municipal Accountant a copy of this report so that an appropriate recording of the assets will be made.

authorize the Local Chief Executive to transfer the completed projects of prior years to
the respective beneficiaries and prepare the Invoice Receipts (IR) Gen. Form No. 30-A to
support the Certificate of Turn-over and Acceptance of Completed Projects to be received
by the recipient schools and barangays.
62
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the Journal Entry Voucher/s
(JEV/s) for the dropping of the affected PPE accounts from the books with the following
attachments: (1) Authority from SB; (2) JEV of the beneficiaries taking up the PPEs
received by them; (3) Invoice Receipt (IR) and (4) Certificate of Turn-over and
Acceptance of Completed Projects duly received by the recipients.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
64
 Exert effort to locate the supporting documents to prove the validity of the claims
and to enforce payment thereof. If found to be no longer payable or without
valid claimants which are two-year-old or over and/or other undocumented
accounts payable irrespective of age, it should be reverted to unappropriated
surplus and funds can be used to finance various projects and programs; and
 Retain copies of supporting documents for all accounts payable recorded.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant to regularly and timely prepare the disbursement voucher and
pertinent supporting documents on the amount auto-debited by the bank from the LGU’s
current account representing its loan payments, which shall be the basis in drawing the
appropriate journal entry.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accounting Office to
submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements together with the Bank Statements,
credit/debit memos and paid checks within the prescribed period provided under COA
Circular No. 96-011 to determine the correctness of the cash in bank balance appearing in
the financial statements.
Likewise, record in the books the P262,795.36 book reconciling items existing in the
submitted bank reconciliation statements. Henceforth, the Municipal Accounting Office
strives to prepare journal entries to record book reconciling items, particularly at the end
of the year to reflect the cash in bank at the adjusted balance in the year-end financial
statements.
We recommended that the MACCO and MTO review the four dormant accounts and
ascertain whether there are still existing valid claims chargeable against such funds. If
none, return the unused fund to the Bureau of Treasury and follow the procedures for the
closing of dormant bank accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the liquidation of payrolls to its
proper expense accounts by considering the nature of the work undertaken concerning the
supporting program design or program of works where it is charged.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjusting entries to
present the amounts received in their appropriate accounts and likewise, instruct the
concerned office to make liquidation of the unexpended amount received from the
Provincial Government of Davao del Norte.
Henceforth, funds received from other local government units should be taken up under
the account “Due to LGUs” and thereafter liquidated to the source agency upon
completion/attainment of the purposes of the fund transfer as required under COA
Circular No. 94-013.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant review and make analysis of the
accounts with negative balances. Draw the appropriate adjusting entries to correct the
said ledgers.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant correct the entries made in the Notes to
Financial Statement and observed the disclosure requirements in accordance with IPSAS
30 for the ensuing year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: 
  
GSO and MACCO to review the CY 2018 inventory balances still existing as of December 31, 2020; verify its existence, conduct actual count and prepare a report thereon to serve as the basis of the MACCO in adjusting/taking-up issuances of affected inventory accounts;
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MACCO to record the said equipment under the appropriate account and to compute and record depreciation and accumulated depreciation.
MACCO to record the said receipt in appropriate income and inventory accounts, and likewise draw necessary entry to expense the same upon submission of the end-users utilization report.
observations.reason: The MACCO did not receive from any offices of the LGU copy of documents for her recording.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 
 
Instruct the concerned departments and offices to indicate in the budget forms the appropriate account for PPAs to be funded pursuant to the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs;
Instruct the LFC to comprehensively review the budget proposals submitted by the different offices;

Require the use of the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 to serve as guide to the budget preparers and discuss the same during the conduct of the budget forum for uniformity and better understanding.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned department heads to regularly submit the monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of MDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC), to the COA Auditor of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Liquidate immediately the cash advances granted to him, and refrain from drawing CAs in his name except for traveling purposes; and
Designate those existing SDOs holding permanent position, for special purpose CAs, if necessary.
We also recommended that the MACCO stop/prohibit granting of cash advances to elective officials, other than for their travelling expenses.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC and its Secretariat to carefully scrutinize the documents submitted by the supplier/s and insure their compliance with the existing procurement rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: 

All department heads to submit a detailed work and financial plan, containing in sufficient detail, every PPA to be included in municipality’s budget; and
The MBO and LFC to ensure that no lump-sum appropriation should be provided in the approved Annual and Supplemental Budgets.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor secure prior approval from the Sangguniang Bayan for every contract to be executed or entered into in instances that it is charged/sourced from the lump-sum appropriations.

We recommended that the Municipality of Talaingod adhere strictly the rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of CAs provided for under COA Circular no. 97-002 and other pertinent rules and regulations.

We also recommended that management direct the concerned officers to see to it that all purchase requests for supplies/materials and catering services pass through the proper procurement procedures undertaken by the BAC to ensure compliance with law and other related rules and regulations.
We further recommended that management at all times ensure that taxes are duly withheld/ remitted as required under the existing BIR rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: 
 
The requisitioning officer/s for the procurement of drugs and medicines be required to certify as to its conformity with the essential drugs list as contained in the PNF, to ensure delivery of effective medicines to the general public;
BAC to require the said certification before drugs/ medicines are subjected to appropriate procurement activities;
MACCO to ensure that payment for drugs and medicines is supported with the required certification.
MACCO to ensure that disbursement vouchers are supported with complete documents and prohibit approving those that are not compliant with relevant guidelines.
observations.reason:
The MPDO stated that they already have policy adopted & signed by LIAC, they missed the provision on requiring the approval of SB and that he will accordingly refer the matter to NHA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the MSWDO to: 
 
Submit justification on the 1,301 mis-targeted SAP cash subsidy beneficiaries as to the 215 with wrong supporting documents based on their status; 1,080 with lacking data presented in their SAC forms; and as to the 6 unqualified beneficiaries;
For IP beneficiaries, submit Certification from NCIP or the tribal chieftain/council of elders that the beneficiaries are IPs and are on subsistence economy;

Require the 4 private employees to submit certification from their respective employers/DOLE that 1) they are “no work no pay” during the ECQ periods; and 2) that they did not receive any cash subsidy under the DOLE amelioration program for private employees;

Maintain an updated database of the municipality’s residents through better coordination with Puroks and Barangays to properly identify possible beneficiaries of social amelioration programs of the government and expedite the delivery of the same.

Require the MSWDO, Acting GSO and MDRRMO to submit a report on the details of the unaccounted relief goods together with the validated list of recipients evidencing proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries;
Require Ms. Cabajar, Mr. Monares and the Inspectorate Team to refund the amount of P34,870.00, as computed above; and
Finally, submit the lacking supporting documents to the audit team otherwise a Notice of Suspension shall be issued to the said transactions.

The Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office submit a written explanation on the non-imposition of liquidated damages on the delays committed by the supplier and collect the same from the supplier.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor fully observe the provisions of Section 2 of PD 1445 in the management of government resources by directing the:

Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) to coordinate with the barangay and the beneficiaries to identify the needs of each PWS that are necessary for it to function. Follow up the application for the water meter and check the extent of damage done to the water pipes due to connection tapping and continue to regularly monitor the effectiveness of all projects.
MEO to coordinate with the MBO and the MACCO, to allocate resources necessary to cover the cost of repair works to be done so that it can be utilized immediately;
MEO and BAC to conscientiously abide by the provision/procedures on termination of contract outlined in the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184;
MEO to devise a catch-up plan to complete the delayed projects to avoid possible wastage of public funds and to strengthen its monitoring activities;
Henceforth, fast track the completion of all projects so that desired benefits are immediately enjoyed by the people.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall instruct the GAD Focal Person to secure copy of a document from the DILG justifying the non-conduct of HGDG. If the same is not available, GAD Focal Person and members of the council shall undergo relevant GAD trainings to capacitate them on the preparation of GAD-PB, GAD-AR and in the administration of the HGDG test. Moreover, ensure that all GAD attributed PPAs is administered through HGDG.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to establish the Municipal Solid Waste Management Fund as a special account in the General Fund and accordingly, prepare the required separate financial reports on the Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Human Resource Management Office to require the CoS and JOs to faithfully record their actual office or work hours. The HRMO shall monitor the same to arrive at the correct man-hours/attendance actually rendered by LGU personnel to avoid overpayment and waste of government funds.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

      We recommended that the management  immediately  secure  the  fidelity
bond for all its accountable officers, including those acting as petty  cash
fund  custodian.   Evaluate  the  amount  of  bond  coverage  and   whenever
necessary, increase their coverage with the Fidelity Bond of the  Bureau  of
Treasury so that accountable officers are adequately and properly bonded.

Recommendation:

      We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
Accounting Office to record all accounts  related  to  the  20%  Development
Fund, particularly the Loans Payable – Domestic so that  data  generated  by
the financial statements are reliable, and credible to avoid inaccurate  and
misleading information.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the  concerned  focal
offices to follow-up the submission of  the  fund  utilization  report  from
TAGEMCO and TWWSC to serve as the  basis  of  the  Municipal  Accountant  in
recording its liquidation.

      Furthermore,  observe  strictly  and  implement  the  guidelines   and
controls under COA Circular No. 2007-001 to ensure that  the  objectives  of
the programs is realized and such services were  extended  to  the  intended
beneficiaries. A copy of the said circular is attached for ready reference.

Recommendations:

      We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
Engineer’s Office to see to it that documentation and  report  forms  to  be
used as SDs  in  the  progress  payments  for  infrastructure  projects  are
complete, proper, sufficiently-labeled and attached to all claims.

      We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall at  all  times
refer to  COA  Circular  2012-001  to  check  whether  or  not  disbursement
vouchers are supported with complete documents and prohibit approving  those
that are not compliant with such guidelines.

      Finally, submit the lacking supporting documents  to  the  audit  team
otherwise a Notice of Suspension shall be issued to the said transactions.

Recommendations:

      We  recommended  that  the  noted  deficiencies  be  rectified  and/or
completed before these  are  accepted  by  the  LGU,  otherwise,  Notice  of
Suspension will be issued for non-conformance with the  approved  plans  and
specifications.

      We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Engineer to conduct strict monitoring on all  projects  implemented  by  the
municipality regardless of whether it  is  implemented  by  contract  or  by
administration.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the  concerned  offices
to ensure that all supporting documents are properly filled up  and  contain
only factual information relative  thereto.  Moreover,  submit  the  lacking
documents as enumerated above.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that the responsible officials  and  employees  of  the
Municipality observe prudence  in  the  discharge  of  their  duties  so  as
government  funds  are  appropriately  safeguarded  such  as   through   the
following measures:

   1. The Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal  Treasurer’s  Office
      submit a written  explanation  on  the  non-imposition  of  liquidated
      damages on the delays committed by the supplier and collect  the  same
      from the supplier; and



   2. The BAC and  Municipal  Accounting  Office  require  the  supplier  to
      provide the  Municipality  the  necessary  warranty  security  on  the
      equipment procured, either in the form  of  cash  or  a  special  bank
      guarantee equivalent to at least 1% of the total contract price.

Recommendation:

      We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct   the   Municipal
Accountant  to  record  all  fund  transfers  received  from  NGAs  to   its
appropriate account as prescribed in the Revised Chart of Accounts for  LGUs
and draw appropriate adjusting entries  to  correct  the  erroneous  entries
made.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OAK Coordinator  to
submit the duly received  list  of  recipient  and  complete  copy  of  duly
approved program design with the needed supplementary information.

      We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant initially record all
procured  supplies  and  materials  for  distribution  to  its   appropriate
inventory account and recognize as expense only upon submission of  the  RIS
and duly received list of recipients to  substantiate  the  distribution  of
the said items.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that the LDRRMC revisit the above mentioned laws, rules
and regulations and refrain from including in the LDRRMF charges which  does
not conformed to such  law,  rules  and  regulations.  Instead,  assess  the
adequacy and availability  of  basic  search,  rescue,  response  and  other
equipment  useful   for   disaster   prevention,   preparedness,   response,
rehabilitation and recovery of the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the individual  SL
to reflect the accurate and reliable balances of these accounts  and  ensure
that only one SL shall be maintained for each supplier/contractor.

      We further recommended that the  Municipal  Accountant  and  Treasurer
cause the immediate transfer of  such  withheld  business  taxes  to  income
account and thereafter be made available/source  for  appropriation  by  the
Municipality.

Recommendation:

      We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
Accounting Office to properly deduct withholding tax on  professional  fees,
talent fees and similar payments to professionals and other  recipients  not
otherwise subject to withholding  tax  on  compensation  as  required  under
subsection (A) of Section 2.57.2 of RR No. 2-98, as amended by  RR  No.  11-
2018 and RR No. 14-2018.

Recommendation:

      We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct   the   Municipal
Accounting Office to trace the original entries of the amounts presented  in
the Due  to/Due  from  Other  Funds  accounts  and  to  make  the  necessary
adjusting/correcting entries to  fully  eliminate  the  difference  of  said
accounts.

Recommendations:

      We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
Accountant to conduct  regular  and  periodic  verification,  analysis,  and
validation of the existence of the receivables and send  demand  letters  to
concerned party/ies to enforce collection of dormant accounts.

      We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant evaluate the  status
of the outstanding receivables and to  request  for  write-off  of  accounts
whose balances are inactive or non-moving for ten  years  or  more  and  its
settlement/collectability could no longer be ascertained in accordance  with
existing auditing rules and regulations.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

   1. Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with  the  provisions  of  COA
      Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization  and  liquidation  of
      cash advances. Stop granting cash advances to officials and  employees
      who have previous unliquidated advances;


   2. Municipal Accountant to send demand letters;


   3. MHRMO to delete  the  name  of  the  official  or  employee  from  the
      subsequent payrolls until such time that the travel cash  advance  has
      been fully liquidated; and


   4. Cause the dissemination of relevant and existing rules and regulations
      on the grant, utilization and liquidation  of  cash  advances  to  all
      officials and employees for proper guidance.

Other Plans as enumerated above.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality effectively review the documents submitted by the Contractor for the “Design and Build” infrastructure projects before approving the same.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit within five (5) days any Variation Order, together with the required supporting documents, to the Audit Team for the timely review of the same.

Furthermore, we recommended that a written comment/explanation should be submitted on the above-mentioned finding for the Audit Team to make a decision in audit.

We recommended that The Municipal Engineer compute the liquidated damages and collect the same from the billings of the contractor and submit the copy of the computation together with the SWA and contractor’s billings used in the computation to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Liquidated damages of P2,728,003.60 were already computed but not yet collected from the contractor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the entire 10% retention money not previously deducted be withheld from the next progress billing of the contractor.
observations.reason: Only P2,099,950.00 retention was deducted from the latest payment made to the contractor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee clearly stipulate in the Notice of Award the posting of the required Performance Security within ten (10) days after receipt of the winning bidder of the NOA and the corresponding penalties in case of failure to do the same. 

Likewise, we recommended that the BAC ensure that the date of receipt by the winning bidder of the Notice of Award is clearly indicated in the NOA to serve as basis in the determination of the winning bidder’s compliance with the Performance Security posting.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee ensure that the date of receipt of the supplier of the Notice to Proceed is clearly indicated in the NTP to serve as basis in the computation of liquidated damages in case the supplier fails to deliver the ordered items.

We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan for CY 2015 and for the years thereafter which is in addition to the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan (LDRRMP) and the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF prepared and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month.
observations.reason: The Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF was not submitted within 15 days after the end of each month. The same was submitted only after year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the transfer of funds to other LGUs be accounted for in accordance with the guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 94-013 wherein a Memorandum of Agreement is entered into by the parties involved and the amount transferred is accounted for as receivable subject to liquidation.
We recommended that a special account be maintained exclusively for the 20% Development Fund for proper monitoring of projects implemented under this fund.

We recommended that the Municipality submit at the beginning of the year to the Supervising Auditor and Audit Team Leader the list of all on-going projects/programs/activities including those non-infrastructure PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year.

Likewise, it is recommended that the implementing offices of the Municipality informed its Supervising Auditor and Audit Team Leader within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.

Furthermore, it is recommended that the Municipality submit the Quarterly Report on Publicized Projects/Programs/Activities on or before the 10th day of the succeeding quarter to the Audit Team.
observations.reason:
The list all on-going PPAs including those non-infra PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year were not submitted.

The SA and the ATL were not informed within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the PPA that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.
Cash advances are still not liquidated immediately.
